items.title: A concise reinforcement learning book
items.oneliner: Free RL book with PyTorch examples covering dynamic programming, Monte Carlo, and PPO from one cloneable repo.

items.title: A low VRAM audio training guide
items.oneliner: Step-by-step tutorial for training a kick drum diffusion model on 6GB VRAM using spectrograms and old hardware.

items.title: A reproducible AI video agent benchmark
items.oneliner: Open framework compares two frontier models making music videos with tool calls, budgets, logs, and runnable code.

items.title: A Go subset that emits C
items.oneliner: Compile a strict subset of Go to C11 for systems projects without Go runtime or garbage collection.

items.title: An interactive linear algebra textbook
items.oneliner: Browser-based linear algebra book uses interactive figures to make matrices, eigenvalues, and Gaussian elimination easier to internalize.

items.title: A practical USB Type C guide
items.oneliner: TI's engineer guide explains USB C power, alt modes, and signaling details you need for real hardware designs.

items.title: A browser tool for vocal notation
items.oneliner: Sing into your browser and export detected notes as MusicXML, ABC, or SVG with metronome-aligned transcription.

items.title: A safer Rust destructuring pattern
items.oneliner: Short Rust tutorial shows how exhaustive destructuring catches missed struct fields at compile time during refactors.

items.title: A classical machine learning detector walkthrough
items.oneliner: Hands-on post builds an LLM text detector with classical ML and explains where perplexity-based approaches fail.

items.title: Microsoft Comic Chat source code
items.oneliner: Historic chat client is now on GitHub for developers who want to inspect, build, or remix early graphical messaging.
